name: Vizsla
description: Hungary's national dog, the Vizsla has been pointing and retrieving alongside Hungarian hunters for over a thousand years, with depictions found in 14th-century stone etchings at royal hunting lodges. Sometimes called the Velcro Vizsla for their intense need for constant human closeness, they form profound bonds and suffer genuine separation anxiety when left alone for long periods. Their rust-colored wash-and-wear coat requires almost no grooming, but their very high energy level demands a committed, athletically active owner.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Epilepsy
conditions_detail.description: Epilepsy is the most common neurological disorder in dogs, characterised by recurrent seizures. Idiopathic (genetic) epilepsy is the most frequent form in young to middle-aged dogs with no underlying structural brain lesion.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Anticonvulsant drugs (phenobarbital, potassium bromide, levetiracetam) control seizures in the majority of cases. Treatment is lifelong. Cluster seizures and status epilepticus require emergency treatment.
prevention_tips: DNA testing available for several breeds
Avoid breeding affected dogs with idiopathic epilepsy

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Lymphoma
conditions_detail.description: Canine lymphoma is one of the most common cancers in dogs, arising from lymphocytes in the lymph nodes, spleen, liver, or bone marrow. Multicentric (involving multiple lymph nodes) is the most common form. It is generally treatable but not curable.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: CHOP-based chemotherapy protocols achieve remission in 60-90% of dogs, with median survival of 12-14 months. Prednisolone alone provides short-term palliation. Targeted therapies are emerging.
prevention_tips: Annual health checks allow earlier detection

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4
